pi_items.source: Verified in setup_task.sh line 24 and used consistently in export_result.sh line 16. This is the directory where the user creates the Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml.

Verified via multiple sources: (1) setup_task.sh line 32 kills xeyes process, (2) export_result.sh lines 42, 60-63 check for xeyes window, (3) verifier.py confirms xeyes is the target process. xeyes is a real X11 demo application from the x11-apps package confirmed via Debian, Gentoo, and Linux documentation sources.
Verified in multiple files: setup_task.sh lines 46, 50 use DISPLAY=:1; export_result.sh lines 8, 61 use DISPLAY=:1. This is the X11 display server identifier where the host's GUI runs.

Verified in export_result.sh line 52 checks for this mount. /tmp/.X11-unix is the standard Linux location for X11 UNIX domain sockets. Docker X11 forwarding commonly mounts this directory from host to container to enable GUI display. Verified via Docker documentation and X11 forwarding guides.
Verified in verifier.py line 13 states 'Pass threshold: 80 points' and line 119 implements this threshold check: 'score >= task_info.get('metadata', {}).get('pass_threshold', 80)'.

privileged_info_summary: The task_id is 'docker_gui_forwarding@1'. The task requires setting up Docker GUI forwarding by creating a Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml in /home/ga/projects/gui-sim directory. The target application is xeyes, a real X11 demo application from the x11-apps package that displays animated eyes following the mouse cursor. The host X11 display server is :1. The Docker container must mount /tmp/.X11-unix from the host to enable X11 socket communication. Success requires: (1) Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml exist, (2) container has X11 socket mount and DISPLAY environment variable configured, (3) container is running, (4) xeyes process runs inside the container, and (5) xeyes window is visible on the host desktop via window manager. The pass threshold is 80 points (out of maximum possible points). No synthetic data is involved; all components (xeyes, X11 display, Docker) are real technologies.
